disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A single very high blood pressure reading (180/100) taken on a public pharmacy machine, with a much lower reading (about 125/80) after sitting quietly at home for 15 minutes
trigger_question_examples: What did the pharmacy machine actually read?
Have you checked your blood pressure again since, and what was it?
How did the reading compare once you were calm at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The pharmacy machine said 180 over 100 and it terrified me. When I got home and sat quietly for about fifteen minutes it was around 125 over 80 - but that first number is all I can think about.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long-standing heightened anxiety and nervousness specifically when around healthcare professionals or medical equipment, with readings that shoot up in clinical settings
trigger_question_examples: Do you tend to get anxious when your blood pressure is being taken or when you're around medical settings?
Have your readings been high before, mainly in clinics or at machines?
How do you feel around doctors, nurses or medical equipment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly, yes - I get incredibly nervous around anything medical. My readings always seem to shoot up at clinics and machines, it's like my body panics.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Completely asymptomatic - no headache, no visual changes, no chest pain, no breathlessness, no focal weakness or speech difficulty
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any headache, chest pain, or shortness of breath right now?
Any changes in your vision, weakness, numbness, or trouble speaking?
Are you having any physical symptoms at all, or is it mainly the worry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that at all - no headache, no chest pain, my vision is fine and I feel physically well. It's really just the fear about that number.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No regular antihypertensive medication and no prior diagnosis of high blood pressure
trigger_question_examples: Are you on any regular medications, particularly for blood pressure?
Have you ever been told you have high blood pressure before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not on any tablets and no one has ever told me I have high blood pressure before today.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Am I about to have a stroke? Should I take something right now to bring my blood pressure down?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Respond with genuine empathy and validation of the fear without dismissing it, while calmly explaining that a single high reading on a pharmacy machine during acute anxiety - especially with no symptoms and a normal reading once calm - is very unlikely to represent an emergency or an imminent stroke. Should NOT advise starting or taking any blood-pressure-lowering medication for an isolated anxiety-driven reading, and should NOT over-promise certainty before completing the history and arranging proper follow-up.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do I need to go to the emergency department right now to be safe?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain clearly why emergency department attendance is not indicated given the reassuring picture (completely asymptomatic, normal reading once calm, classic white-coat pattern), while still validating the concern, providing clear emergency safety-netting for what WOULD change that, and arranging home/ambulatory blood pressure monitoring with routine GP follow-up rather than leaving her with no plan.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient repeatedly circles back to the single high pharmacy number and fear of a stroke rather than taking in the reassurance - tests whether the AI Doctor can gently redirect and gather the needed history (symptoms, the calm home reading, the situational anxiety pattern) without either dismissing the anxiety or being pulled into over-escalation.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: But 180 over 100 is so high - I just keep thinking I'm going to have a stroke tonight, are you sure I shouldn't go to hospital?
